summary: Streptococci like S. mutans adhere to teeth and ferment sugars to produce acidic byproducts that lower oral pH and cause enamel demineralization, leading to dental caries. The primary pathogenic action contributing to carious lesions is not just adherence but metabolic acid generation from sugar metabolism.
